assertion_results.text: Output file AGENTS.md exists in project root
Identifies project as a skill repository
assertion_results.evidence: Header: 'Type: Agent Skill (compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot)'. Architecture section references '.claude-plugin/plugin.json # Skill plugin manifest'.
Architecture tree: 'skills/cli-tools/ -> SKILL.md # Skill definition and agent workflows'. Key Concepts section: 'The skill registers a PostToolUse hook'.

assertion_results.text: Lists scripts if any exist in the repo
assertion_results.evidence: Architecture tree lists: install_tool.sh, check_environment.sh, detect_project_type.sh, auto_update.sh, install_composer.sh, detect_missing_tool.py. Development Guidelines section references these scripts with usage examples.

assertion_results.text: Does NOT contain irrelevant build/compile instructions
assertion_results.evidence: No build/compile instructions. Development Guidelines cover adding tools, modifying installation logic, modifying detection hook, CI quality, and versioning -- all relevant to the skill repo type.

assertion_results.text: Mentions the skill's purpose from SKILL.md description
assertion_results.evidence: Project Overview: 'CLI Tools Skill provides automatic CLI tool management for AI agents. It detects missing tools from shell errors, installs them via optimal package managers, and audits project environments for missing or outdated tooling.' Hook-Based Detection section elaborates on the mechanism.

assertion_results.text: Structure is concise (under 100 lines for a simple skill repo)
assertion_results.evidence: 137 lines. Similar to with_skill, this is a complex skill repo. However, it exceeds the 100-line threshold.

qualitative.structure_quality: Good. Has clear sections: Overview, Architecture, Key Concepts, Development Guidelines, CI and Quality, Versioning, Coding Standards. However, reads more like a README/dev-guide than an agent-optimized AGENTS.md.
qualitative.conciseness: Reasonable at 137 lines. The architecture tree is thorough. However, Key Concepts section (Hook-Based Detection, Tool Catalog, Installation Priority, Project Type Detection) overlaps with what SKILL.md already documents.
qualitative.accuracy: Good. Script paths, architecture tree, and descriptions are accurate. Hook mechanism correctly described. Installation priority order stated.
qualitative.usefulness_for_agents: Moderate. Lacks golden samples, utilities table, heuristics for quick decisions, and clear boundaries (always/ask/never). An agent would know the architecture but not the patterns to follow. No quick-reference tables for common tasks.
